title: iEEG_visual
description: This intracranial EEG dataset comprises recordings from 2 subjects performing a visual task involving presentation of gratings and noise patterns. The dataset was collected to investigate stimulus-dependent gamma oscillations in human visual cortex and the relationship between neuronal synchrony and the blood-oxygen-level dependent response. Raw data are organized according to the BIDS specification and include electrocorticographic recordings, event timing metadata, and visual stimulus images.
methods_description: Subjects were presented with visual stimuli (high-contrast vertical gratings with varying duty cycles and noise patterns) displayed on a computer screen spanning approximately 25×25 degrees of visual angle. Subjects maintained fixation on a centrally-located dot that alternated between red and green at random intervals. Subject 1 performed a button-press response task upon color changes, while Subject 2 maintained fixation without manual responses. Intracranial EEG data were collected using the BrainVision data format.
